name: Eekso — Session Open
dmNotes: THE OPENER. Order: poem → welcome + character intros → recap → where we're at → the bridge. The poem is built like 'The Priest's Descent' (the Shipton poem) — same couplets, and the LAST TWO LINES deliberately echo its ending ('Beneath the lake, beneath the roar...'). Both are Torm stories. See if Eddy's player catches it — don't point it out. Recap covers Session 25 (the courier hulk, the bottle, IT CONSUMES, the bird, the roar). End on the exterior reveal of Eekso from the bridge, then switch to 'The Broken Foyer' when they go through the doors.
scriptText:
# Session Open

## The Poem

*The water carved this place alone,*
*Before a name was ever known.*
*It does not rage. It does not war.*
*It only takes a little more.*

*Then men came down and built their keep,*
*And laid their dead, and dared to sleep.*
*They raised their flags. They barred the door.*
*The water ran beneath the floor.*

*A hundred masters held these halls,*
*A hundred banners on the walls,*
*And every soul who came before*
*Went down the dark, and is no more.*

*Beneath the bridge, beneath the roar,*
*The river keeps them, forevermore.*

> [!dm] Last couplet echoes the Shipton poem on purpose. Don't point it out — see if anyone catches it.

## Welcome

> [!dm] The usual, your voice:
>
> - Welcome back adventurers, to the dark subterranean depths of Elor
> - Tradition: character names + any fun notes. Jake, start it off.
> - Housekeeping — tonight is a dungeon crawl, start to finish

## The Recap

{{music:recap}}

Last session, you fought the umber hulk on the riverbank. [[pc-varen|Varen]] got knocked out, [[cumb]] fed him a potion, and you brought the thing down — and as it died, it half-shifted into something else and let out a roar so loud it literally burst some eardrums. Then you did what any normal group of adventurers would do: Cumb and [[pc-felson|Felson]] cut off a claw to cook, and [[pc-eddy|Eddy]] and Varen cracked the skull open to look at the brain. Eddy's Divine Sense picked up something almost divine — but strange. Foreign. Then the body melted. The claw melted too. Sorry Felson, no hulk dinner. Felson did manage to catch some of the liquid in an empty potion bottle before the river washed the rest away, and he's still got it.

Downriver you found an old shed where somebody had been living, and studying something. Carved into the desk, over and over, in Elvish: **IT CONSUMES.** And outside, a broken slave collar.

Then you heard weird sounds in the dark — meows, moos, someone calling for help — and a weird colorful bird walked straight up to Eddy like it already knew him. And it stayed.

And then, from behind you: that same roar. Again. Closer.

This is where we begin.

## Where We're At

{{music:run}}

- You ran — downriver, no discussion, the roar somewhere behind you in the dark

- The tunnel got narrower, the water got faster and louder, and the spray in the air made everything cold and wet

- Then the cave opened up, and you saw where all this water has been going

## The Bridge

{{music:reveal}} {{amb:nox-stream|vol=40%|in=3s}} {{amb:fap-cave-wind|vol=30%|in=3s}}

The river drops away in front of you, white and roaring, down into the dark. The sound fills the whole cave. You can feel it in the bridge under your feet — a bridge of old black stone, slick with spray, crossing right over the falls.

And on the far side, filling the cave wall to wall, is a fortress.

It's built straight across the river. The towers run into the cave rock like they grew there. The walls have been patched over and over with different stone by different hands. The river runs in underneath it through big stone grates, like teeth — and here's the thing. You can't hear it come out the other side.

Behind you, the roar sounds again. Closer.

Ahead of you, at the end of the bridge, a pair of huge doors sit open. Just a hand's width.

What are you doing?

> [!dm] This is **Eekso**.
>
> - The roar does not follow them past the bridge. It's NOT part of Eekso — just another beast of the deep (maybe a young dragon, [[grill-queue]]). It never comes inside.
> - If they inspect the doors: ancient Thundrak Dûrn hinges, but the **bar is new wood**. Someone maintains this door.
> - Through the doors → switch to **'Eekso — The Broken Foyer'**

ideas.text: The doors stand a hand's width open — an invitation dressed as an accident
Ancient hinges, new wooden bar. Someone maintains this door.
The river runs INTO the fortress through grates like teeth, and you can't hear it come out

entities.name: Eekso, from the bridge
entities.note: A fortress built across the river, fused into the cave rock. The doors stand ajar.

entities.name: The roar behind
entities.note: Sounds once more at the bridge, then never follows. Unrelated to Eekso — some other beast, maybe a young dragon (grill-queue).
